mnemonics: PAP: For a Regular Stochastic Matrix, some Power Always has All Positive entries.
keyPoints: A stochastic matrix is regular if some power of it (P^k) has all strictly positive entries (no zeros).
Regular Markov chains are guaranteed to have a unique steady-state distribution, regardless of the starting state.
The steady-state vector π can be found by solving the equation πP = π, along with the condition that the sum of π's components equals 1.
commonMistakes: Assuming that all stochastic matrices are regular. Many are not (e.g., matrices with absorbing states).

Confusing a regular matrix (some power is positive) with a matrix that is positive (all entries > 0) from the start. A positive matrix is regular, but a regular matrix only needs to be positive after some number of steps.
